userPrompt: All this is built out I want you to send each zone agent into its owned nodes and clean the code. make sure functions are reused, there isnt any deprecated code still with the project, make sure its not over engineered. Report findings and changes back to the conductor.
summary: All this is built out I want you to send each zone agent into its owned nodes and clean the code. make sure functions ar
conductorDecisions: {"type":"assign","assignments":[{"zoneId":"Renderer-UX","taskId":"t-cleanup-ux","body":"Pure code cleanup of your owned files in src/renderer/ (App.tsx, types.ts, components/layout/*, components/nodes/*, components/dispatch/*, components/palette/*, and renderer-side terminal/assistant/files panels). Goals: (1) remove dead/deprecated code — v4 mailbox refs, legacy 'finished' lifecycle, unused exports, commented-out blocks, deliveryWarningMs/taskTimeoutMs leftovers; (2) dedupe — pull repeated logic into existing helpers, do NOT introduce new abstractions unless 3+ duplicates; (3) drop over-engineering — speculative options, unused params/props, premature interfaces. CONSTRAINTS: no behavior changes, no renames that ripple to other zones, no new features, do not touch files outside src/renderer/. If you spot bugs or larger refactors worth doing, FLAG them in your report — do not implement. When done: run `npm run build` from the repo root to verify TypeScript + Vite. Write a report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Renderer-UX.md listing: files touched, what was removed/deduped (one line each), build result, and a 'Follow-ups' section. Then append a `done` activity line with a 1-line summary."},{"zoneId":"Electron-Platform","taskId":"t-cleanup-platform","body":"Pure code cleanup of your owned files: src/main/index.ts, src/preload/index.ts, and any canvas-persistence / file-watcher code in main not owned by Dispatch-Coordination or Zone-Runtime-Fleet. Goals: (1) remove dead/deprecated code — v4 leftovers, unused IPC handlers, commented-out blocks; (2) dedupe — reuse existing helpers, no new abstractions unless 3+ duplicates; (3) drop over-engineering — unused params, speculative options. CONSTRAINTS: do NOT touch src/main/terminals.ts, src/main/orchestrator/*, src/main/runtimes/*, src/main/sessionCapture.ts, src/main/dispatchCapture.ts (other zones own those). No behavior changes. Flag follow-ups, don't implement. When done: run `npm run build` from repo root. Write a report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Electron-Platform.md (files touched, removed/deduped one-liners, build result, Follow-ups). Append a `done` activity line."},{"zoneId":"Dispatch-Coordination","taskId":"t-cleanup-dispatch","body":"Pure code cleanup of: src/main/terminals.ts, src/main/dispatchCapture.ts, and ALL of src/main/orchestrator/ (activity.ts, state.ts, status.ts, conductor.ts, scheduler.ts, workspace.ts, dispatch.ts, prompts/conductor.ts, prompts/zone.ts, prompts/solo.ts). Goals: (1) remove dead/deprecated code — v4 mailbox/script refs, legacy 'finished' lifecycle, deliveryWarningMs/taskTimeoutMs if any survive, unused exports, sigil/screen-grid leftovers; (2) dedupe — reuse existing helpers across orchestrator + terminals, no new abstractions unless 3+ duplicates; (3) drop over-engineering — speculative options, unused params, premature interfaces. CONSTRAINTS: do NOT touch src/main/runtimes/* or src/main/sessionCapture.ts (Zone-Runtime-Fleet owns those). Do NOT touch src/main/index.ts or preload (Electron-Platform). No behavior changes — protocolVersion=5 contracts, activity-log JSONL schema, pty.write submit semantics, user-control lock all preserved exactly. Flag follow-ups, don't implement. When done: run `npm run build` from repo root. Write a report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Dispatch-Coordination.md (files touched, removed/deduped one-liners, build result, Follow-ups). Append a `done` activity line."},{"zoneId":"Zone-Runtime-Fleet","taskId":"t-cleanup-runtime","body":"Pure code cleanup of: src/main/runtimes/* (types.ts, claude.ts, codex.ts, gemini.ts, opencode.ts, fold.ts, index.ts) and src/main/sessionCapture.ts. Also audit ARCHITECT/ artifact code paths (workspace.ts is Dispatch-Coordination's, but the per-runtime session-dir scanning + capture polling logic belongs to you). Goals: (1) remove dead/deprecated code — unused adapter methods, dead branches per runtime, v4 leftovers; (2) dedupe — pull common logic across the 4 adapters into fold.ts or a small shared helper if it appears in 3+ adapters; (3) drop over-engineering — speculative SpawnArgs/ResumeArgs fields nobody passes, unused timeouts. CONSTRAINTS: do NOT touch src/main/terminals.ts or src/main/orchestrator/* (Dispatch-Coordination). Preserve the RuntimeAdapter interface contract exactly — every method signature stays. No behavior changes. Flag follow-ups, don't implement. When done: run `npm run build` from repo root. Write a report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Zone-Runtime-Fleet.md (files touched, removed/deduped one-liners, build result, Follow-ups). Append a `done` activity line."}]}

{"type":"noop","reason":"Electron-Platform complete with build green. Three zones still working — wait for them before emitting final. Cross-zone follow-ups (env.d.ts, observability IPC, sessionCapture dup key) noted for final summary."}

{"type":"noop","reason":"Zone-Runtime-Fleet complete with build green and meaningful dedup (fold helper, forEachCodexDayDir). Two zones still working — Renderer-UX and Dispatch-Coordination. Wait before final. Note: readRecord-drops-model follow-up may be a real bug worth surfacing in final summary."}

{"type":"final","summary":"Cleanup complete across 4 zones, all builds green, no behavior changes.

**Renderer-UX** — see ARCHITECT/outputs/Renderer-UX.md.

**Electron-Platform** — Removed 2 dead IPC handlers (get-home-dir, read-file) and their preload exposures (readFile, getHomeDir); zero renderer callers. Rest of index.ts/preload already tight.

**Dispatch-Coordination** — terminals.ts: dropped dead Session.events/eventSeq/tail, ZoneEvent type, pushEvent, TAIL_MAX_BYTES; trimmed v4 historic comments; simplified setLifecycle. dispatch.ts: removed wireScheduler placeholder + unused dispatchContext field. activity.ts: fixed stale printf comment. state.ts: removed dead 'harness' role, 'resumed' TaskStatus, no-op parseValue. Protocol v5 contracts (activity-log JSONL, pty.write submit, user-control lock) preserved exactly.

**Zone-Runtime-Fleet** — Added foldComposeSystemAndUser to fold.ts; codex/gemini/opencode share it (3 duplicate composeSystemAndUser bodies removed). Extracted forEachCodexDayDir, deduping two ~30-line YYYY/MM/DD walks in sessionCapture.ts. Removed unused ZoneSession back-compat alias. RuntimeAdapter interface preserved exactly.

**Cross-zone follow-ups flagged (NOT implemented):**
- env.d.ts drift in renderer (Renderer-UX area)
- Possibly-unused observability IPC (needs cross-zone decision)
- sessionCapture.ts duplicate-key warning
- readRecord drops the `model` field that callers depend on — likely a real bug, worth a follow-up dispatch
- Duplicate `hasTrustDialogAccepted` literal
- Legacy migration path possibly removable
- Capture timeouts could route through harnessTimeouts instead of hardcoded values
- opencode session polling spawns a PTY per tick (perf)

Full per-zone reports in ARCHITECT/outputs/{Renderer-UX,Electron-Platform,Dispatch-Coordination,Zone-Runtime-Fleet}.md."}

{"type":"assign","assignments":[{"zoneId":"Renderer-UX","taskId":"t-docs-ux","body":"Owner of docs/frontend.md AND README.md. Audit both against current renderer code (src/renderer/**) and patch drift so the docs reflect shipped behavior. Reference ARCHITECT/outputs/Renderer-UX.md (your last cleanup report) for the recent removal list — anything mentioned as removed there should not appear as a current feature in either doc. KILL LIST to scrub from these docs (if mentioned): v4 mailbox/scripts, 'finished' lifecycle state, deliveryWarningMs, taskTimeoutMs, ZoneEvent/pushEvent, screen-grid/sigil scanning, identity-memo patterns, Session.tail, 'harness' role, 'resumed' TaskStatus, get-home-dir/read-file IPC + getHomeDir/readFile preload, capturePendingIds, paneCount, autoSaveId. CONSTRAINTS: no speculative/aspirational content, doc must match shipped code; no edits to other zones' docs (docs/orchestration.md, docs/agent-behavior.md, CLAUDE.md) — flag drift you spot there in your report. README.md is user-facing, keep it accessible; docs/frontend.md is technical. When done, write report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Renderer-UX.md (overwrite previous): files touched, one-liner per correction, sections removed, cross-doc drift flagged for other owners, follow-ups. Append a `done` activity line."},{"zoneId":"Electron-Platform","taskId":"t-docs-platform","body":"Owner of CLAUDE.md (AGENTS.md is a symlink, edits to CLAUDE.md cover both). Audit against current main process + IPC code (src/main/index.ts, src/preload/index.ts) AND consume the cross-doc drift flagged by the other 3 zones in their reports — your role is to consolidate CLAUDE.md corrections from all zones since CLAUDE.md spans the whole project. Reference ARCHITECT/outputs/Electron-Platform.md (your last cleanup report) for your removed-IPC list. KILL LIST to scrub from CLAUDE.md if mentioned: v4 mailbox/scripts, 'finished' lifecycle state, deliveryWarningMs, taskTimeoutMs, ZoneEvent/pushEvent, screen-grid/sigil scanning, Session.tail/eventSeq, 'harness' role, 'resumed' TaskStatus, get-home-dir/read-file IPC, getHomeDir/readFile preload, wireScheduler placeholder, ZoneSession back-compat alias. ORDER OF OPS: wait for the 3 other zones to land their reports first (your turn will be re-driven by the harness when they finish), then read their 'cross-doc drift flagged' sections and apply CLAUDE.md edits. CONSTRAINTS: no speculative content; CLAUDE.md must match shipped code exactly; do NOT edit AGENTS.md directly (it's a symlink). Verify symlink integrity at end (`ls -la AGENTS.md` should show -> CLAUDE.md). Write report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Electron-Platform.md with: sections corrected, sections removed, source of each correction (which zone flagged it or self-found), follow-ups. Append `done` activity line."},{"zoneId":"Dispatch-Coordination","taskId":"t-docs-dispatch","body":"Owner of docs/orchestration.md. Also responsible for DELETIONS: rm architect-plans/headless-transition.md, rm orch-mason-progress.md, and rmdir architect-plans/ if empty afterward. Audit docs/orchestration.md against current orchestrator + terminals code (src/main/terminals.ts, src/main/orchestrator/**, src/main/dispatchCapture.ts) and patch drift. Reference ARCHITECT/outputs/Dispatch-Coordination.md (your last cleanup report) for removed-symbol list. KILL LIST to scrub from docs/orchestration.md if mentioned: v4 mailbox transport, mailbox-listen.sh, ARCHITECT/scripts, 'finished' lifecycle, deliveryWarningMs, taskTimeoutMs, ZoneEvent type, pushEvent, Session.events/eventSeq/tail, TAIL_MAX_BYTES, screen-grid/sigil scanning, 'harness' role enum, 'resumed' TaskStatus, no-op parseValue, wireScheduler placeholder, dispatchContext field. Doc must accurately describe: protocolVersion=5, activity-log JSONL contract (7 ActivityEvent kinds: task-received/progress/ask/answer/done/failed/note), pty.write two-step submit (text + 120ms + \\r), user-control lock semantics, scheduler responsibilities, ConductorDecision union (assign/answer/final/noop), per-task state machine (pending → dispatched → in-progress → {done|failed|blocked → in-progress}), 15s status tick, multi-signal staleness, harnessTimeouts (idleThresholdMs, staleEscalationMs). CONSTRAINTS: no speculation; flag CLAUDE.md drift in your report for Electron-Platform; do not edit other zones' docs. Write report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Dispatch-Coordination.md (overwrite): files touched, deletions performed, one-liner per correction, sections removed, cross-doc drift for Electron-Platform, follow-ups. Append `done` activity line."},{"zoneId":"Zone-Runtime-Fleet","taskId":"t-docs-runtime","body":"Owner of docs/agent-behavior.md. Audit against current runtime + session code (src/main/runtimes/**, src/main/sessionCapture.ts). Doc must accurately describe: the 4 supported runtimes (claude/codex/gemini/opencode) with their current uneven feature support, RuntimeAdapter interface (supportsSystemPromptFlag, buildSpawnArgs, buildResumeArgs, composeSystemAndUser, snapshotSessions, captureNewSession, revalidateSession), per-runtime CLI flags (claude --append-system-prompt + --resume; codex resume <id> subcommand + --no-alt-screen -a never -s workspace-write; gemini --approval-mode yolo + --prompt-interactive + --resume; opencode --session <id> never bare --continue, --prompt), session storage paths per runtime (~/.claude/projects/, ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/, ~/.gemini/tmp/, opencode list-via-PTY), system-prompt fold via <<SYSTEM>>...<<END>> for non-Claude runtimes through shared foldComposeSystemAndUser. KILL LIST: v4 mailbox refs, deprecated lifecycle states, ZoneSession alias, any runtime branches that no longer exist, screen-grid/sigil scanning. CONSTRAINTS: no speculation about future runtime support; describe only shipped capabilities; flag CLAUDE.md drift in your report for Electron-Platform; do not edit other zones' docs. Write report to ARCHITECT/outputs/Zone-Runtime-Fleet.md (overwrite): one-liner per correction, sections removed, cross-doc drift for Electron-Platform, follow-ups. Append `done` activity line."}]}

{"type":"final","summary":"Canvas audit complete across all 7 zones. architect-canvas.json was NOT edited (audit-only).

**Confirmed drift requiring an edit (1 item):**
- `ipc-bridge.data.specs` lists `readFile` which was removed from the preload during the cleanup pass. Source: Electron-Platform.

**Flagged but judgment calls (no auto-edit):**
- Runtime Adapters component sits inside Dispatch-Coordination's zone bbox, but the actual code lives in src/main/runtimes/ — Zone-Runtime-Fleet's territory. Could be re-parented or left as-is if the canvas treats 'Runtime Adapters' as a concept owned at the dispatch level. (Dispatch-Coordination)
- `dispatch-record → scheduler` edge is unidirectional in canvas; in code, the Scheduler also writes back via `setDispatchPendingTasks` + `appendDispatchConductorDecision`. Adding a reverse edge or marking bidirectional would be more accurate. (Dispatch-Coordination)
- The three comm zones (Conductor-Agent, Scheduler-Harness, Zone-Fleet) and their `comm-*` components shadow components Dispatch-Coordination already owns (Conductor PTY ↔ conductor, Scheduler ↔ scheduler, fs.watch + pty.write Delivery ↔ activity-log + pty-orchestrator, Zone PTY/Log ↔ agent-pool + activity-log). Same source files double-counted as 'agent-level' vs. 'mechanism-level' views. Independently confirmed by Scheduler-Harness, Conductor-Agent, Zone-Fleet, and Dispatch-Coordination. Decide whether the dual view is intentional or one set should be removed.
- Zone PTY A/B labels read as a fixed two-zone design; in code the fleet is N-zone. Zone-Fleet flagged this as mild and acceptable since component descriptions already note N-zone reality.

**Optional gaps flagged (not drift, product calls):**
- Renderer-UX: Files Panel, Settings Panel, Canvas Palette are shipped top-level renderer surfaces but not represented as components.
- Zone-Runtime-Fleet: Skills Library spec attributes skill loading to prompt builders, but the actual loader is `readSkillContent` in terminals.ts:892 — outcome is identical, source location is just slightly off in the description.

**Zero-drift zones:** Renderer-UX, Conductor-Agent, Zone-Runtime-Fleet (cosmetic only).

Full per-zone reports under `ARCHITECT/outputs/<zone>.md` → '## Canvas audit (t-canvas-*)' section."}
